Tests the behavior of RegExp.prototype.compile with an empty array argument.

# Purpose
This code tests the behavior of the `compile` method on the `RegExp.prototype` object. It assigns the result of calling `RegExp.prototype.compile` with an empty array as an argument to the variable `result`. The `assert` statement then checks if `result` is `undefined`, which is the expected outcome. This script is a simple test case to verify that the `compile` method behaves as expected when given an empty array.
